judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with essentially equivalent, correct analysis; they are comparable.

report_1.evidence:
`config/scoring_config.yaml` sets `expected_label_field: label`.
- But every record in `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` now has the key `gold_label`, not `label`... So `record.get("label")` returns `None` for every single record... The fix is a one-line change to `ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`: change `expected_label_field: label` to `expected_label_field: gold_label`.

report_2.evidence: `config/scoring_config.yaml` sets `expected_label_field: label`, but every record in `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` now has its gold label stored under the key `gold_label` (not `label`). So `record.get("label")` always returns `None`... The fix is a one-line change to **`ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`**

report_1.evidence: The regression began on **2026-06-17**, the date of commit `53551e8` ("chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)")... `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` shows accuracy at 0.91/0.90/0.92 (cycling) every day from 2026-05-20 through 2026-06-16, then exactly 0.00 every day from 2026-06-17 onward

report_2.evidence: The regression began on **2026-06-17**, introduced by commit `53551e8` — `"chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)"`. That commit renamed the label key in every record of `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl`... `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` shows accuracy at 0.90–0.92 every day through 2026-06-16, then exactly 0.00 starting 2026-06-17

report_1.evidence: No, I disagree, and the drop is trivially and deterministically reproducible... `score_one.py` never loads the eval set or a gold label at all... It has no code path that reads `expected_label_field` or compares against `gold_label`/`label`, so it is structurally incapable of exposing this bug. The correct way to reproduce: run the actual nightly batch scorer, not the single-item tool

report_2.evidence: No. The prior investigation tested the wrong code path. `score_one.py` (`score_single`) never reads `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` or `config/scoring_config.yaml` at all... It is structurally incapable of exposing this bug... The correct way to reproduce this bug is to run the actual batch entry point (`pipeline/score_batch.py`...) against the real eval fixture and config

report_1.evidence: Swapping "the model" would touch code that was never implicated and would leave `expected_label_field: label` pointing at a key that no longer exists in the eval set — accuracy would stay at 0.00 regardless of which classifier version runs.

report_2.evidence: Rolling back "the model" would leave `expected_label_field: label` unchanged, `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` still keyed on `gold_label`, and accuracy would remain exactly 0.0.
